name: checker-town
title: Checker Town
description: The place where the story begins. Checker Town is a struggling community where most people are born, stay, and never leave -- not because they don't want to, but because the odds are stacked against them.
features: Houses and buildings are constructed from enormous checker pieces that fell from the sky long ago -- no one fully understands why or how | Checkers is the dominant game; chess is known but rarely taken seriously | The community is poor and hope is in short supply | Close enough to see what lies beyond, far enough that getting there feels impossible
who_lives_here: The narrator, the Rival, and the Best Friend all call Checker Town home. Princess does too. It is the place they are all, in different ways, trying to escape -- or make peace with.
story_role: Checker Town is the world the story starts in and the world the story keeps pulling back to. It represents everything the narrator is fighting against, and everything he risks losing by leaving.

name: chess-city
title: Chess City
description: The destination. Chess City is well-to-do, prosperous, and deliberately difficult to reach. You cannot simply move there -- you have to earn your way in.
features: Located across the sea from Checker Town | Entry requires winning chess tournaments; there is no other way in | Visitors and workers must pass through security checkpoints and be Tagged with a symbol upon each visit | Family and friends of Checker Town residents often live here, creating a painful separation for those left behind
who_lives_here: The Rival's entire family lives in Chess City. This is the central wound of his character -- and the engine behind everything he does. Chess City Elementary, where the narrator eventually works, is located here.
story_role: Chess City is the prize, the promise, and the obstacle. It represents a better life -- but one that has to be fought for, and one that comes with its own costs once you arrive.

name: shogi-island
title: Shogi Island
description: A nearby island where chess is a footnote and Shogi is everything. Shogi Island operates by its own rules and its own language.
features: Located near both Checker Town and Chess City | Shogi is the dominant game; chess is rarely played | Everyone on the island speaks Japanese | Has its own distinct culture, separate from the chess-and-checkers world of the mainland
story_role: Shogi Island exists at the edges of the story -- a reminder that the world is bigger than the chess-versus-checkers divide. Whether the narrator or Princess passes through here remains to be seen.

name: mystery-city
description: Nobody knows its real name. Nobody knows exactly where it is. But everyone has heard the rumors.
features: It is the Capital of Chess -- the origin of everything | The checker pieces that built Checker Town fell from somewhere, and this city is where they came from | Details beyond that are scarce, disputed, or deliberately buried
story_role: The mystery city lurks at the edge of the story's mythology. It may be the answer to questions the characters don't yet know how to ask.

name: chess-city-elementary
title: Chess City Elementary
description: A school inside Chess City where the narrator finds work -- first as a janitor, and eventually as a chess teacher.
features: Located in Chess City, which means the narrator must pass through the security checkpoint and get Tagged every single shift | The job sits at an uncomfortable intersection: it is inside the city he wants to belong to, but accessed only as a worker | The options facing the narrator are narrow: Chess City Elementary, college (which feels like a dead end), or the Sand Mines (which are too far from Princess)
story_role: Chess City Elementary is where the narrator's journey and his teaching life begin to converge. It is a place of contradiction -- close to the dream, but still at arm's length.

name: sand-mines
title: The Sand Mines
description: Hard labor at the far end of a bad road. The Sand Mines are one of the few employment options available to Checker Town citizens -- and one of the hardest to escape.
features: Workforce is made up almost entirely of Checker Town residents | Run by corporate interests who maintain tight control over workers | Workers are either physically constrained or find themselves unable to leave for other reasons | Located too far from Checker Town for the narrator to stay close to Princess -- a significant factor in his calculus
mysteries: There are rumors of a portal -- called the *pu-ri-n-se-suuuu* portal -- that may connect the Sand Mines to somewhere else entirely. What lies on the other side is unknown.
story_role: The Sand Mines represent one of the worst-case outcomes for someone from Checker Town. The narrator may end up here. Whether he does -- and whether he can leave -- is part of what drives the story forward.
